findings.evidence: `CircleFundamentalGroup.lean:75-80` uses `Deck.addCircleMulEquivInt hp`; `UniversalCover/AddCircle.lean:141-143` has `addCircleMulEquiv : Multiplicative (zmultiples p) ≃* Deck ((↑) : 𝕜 → AddCircle p)`, while `UniversalCover/AddCircle.lean:174-176` shows the ℤ form is only a specialization requiring `hp`.
findings.fix: First state/prove the general equivalence `FundamentalGroup (AddCircle p) x ≃* Multiplicative (zmultiples p)` using `Deck.addCircleMulEquiv`, then derive the current `Multiplicative ℤ` theorem by composing with `intEquivZMultiples hp`.
findings.issue: `fundamentalGroupMulEquivOfAddCircle` assumes `hp : ¬ IsOfFinAddOrder p` and targets `Multiplicative ℤ`, but the preceding deck API already proves the more general deck computation with target `Multiplicative (zmultiples p)`.

summary: The circle specialization is at the right level, but the generic AddCircle theorem jumps straight to the infinite-cyclic case instead of exposing the natural period-subgroup statement already supported by the deck API.
